description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust base. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are set at a fixed tilt to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from metal, possibly steel or aluminum, and includes a patterned surface that may serve as a protective or structural platform. The rear section of the unit is elevated, possibly housing electrical components or providing additional support. No visible cabling or inverter is apparent from the exterior, suggesting these elements may be internally integrated. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility spaces.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal base with a patterned platform and elevated rear section. The panels are dark and set at a fixed tilt, designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dark solar panel surface and the patterned base, with the panel tilt clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the elevated rear section and the depth of the base, highlighting the panel's tilt and support structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the panel and the full height of the elevated section, with the patterned base extending forward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the panel tilt and the structural details of the base and rear elevation.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame with a patterned base platform
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 1 panel, compact rectangular arrangement
